name: Serengeti National Park
description: Experience the Great Migration, sweeping savannah sunsets, and year-round predator action in one of Africa's most iconic safari landscapes.
shortDescription: Migration drama, classic safaris, and huge wildlife density.
visaNotes: Visa on arrival or e-visa depending on nationality.

name: Masai Mara National Reserve
description: Kenya's flagship safari reserve, known for big cats, dramatic river crossings, and high-end camp experiences.
shortDescription: Big cats, migration crossings, and premium safari camps.
visaNotes: Electronic travel authorisation required for many visitors.

name: Victoria Falls
description: A thunderous natural wonder pairing waterfall viewpoints, helicopter rides, rafting, and sunset cruises.
shortDescription: Epic waterfall power with adventure and river sunsets.
visaNotes: KAZA UniVisa can simplify travel across Zimbabwe and Zambia for eligible travelers.

name: Sossusvlei
description: Towering dunes, surreal desert textures, and one of Africa's most photogenic sunrise landscapes.
shortDescription: Red dunes, sunrise climbs, and minimalist desert beauty.
visaNotes: Visa-free for many nationalities; always verify latest immigration rules.

name: Cape Town
description: A city where mountains, beaches, wine country, and design-forward urban experiences meet.
shortDescription: Atlantic beaches, Table Mountain, food, and city energy.
visaNotes: Visa-free for many travelers for tourism stays.

name: Marrakech
description: A vibrant cultural destination with riads, souks, gardens, architecture, and easy access to the Atlas Mountains.
shortDescription: Colorful medina life, design hotels, food, and culture.
visaNotes: Visa-free for many tourism markets for shorter stays.

name: Zanzibar Archipelago
description: Beach escape meets Swahili heritage, reef adventures, spice farms, and boutique island stays.
shortDescription: Beaches, Stone Town, spice trails, and warm Indian Ocean water.
visaNotes: Same Tanzanian visa regime as the mainland.

name: Ngorongoro Crater
description: An ancient caldera delivering concentrated wildlife sightings, crater-rim scenery, and a strong Big Five appeal.
shortDescription: A natural amphitheater with dense wildlife and dramatic scenery.
visaNotes: Falls under Tanzania visa rules.

name: Seychelles
description: A jewel-like Indian Ocean archipelago of 115 islands, renowned for granite boulder beaches, turquoise lagoons, coral reefs, and exceptional luxury resorts.
shortDescription: Granite boulders, turquoise lagoons, and barefoot luxury.
visaNotes: All nationalities receive a free visitor's permit on arrival for up to 30 days.

name: Mauritius
description: A stunning volcanic island in the Indian Ocean offering emerald lagoons, world-class golf, luxury beach resorts, and a rich multicultural heritage.
shortDescription: Lagoon beaches, luxury resorts, and vibrant Creole culture.
visaNotes: Visa-free for most nationalities for stays up to 90 days.

name: Dakar & Gorée Island
description: A dynamic West African capital where vibrant street art, Afrobeat rhythms, and Teranga hospitality fuse with the haunting history of Gorée Island's UNESCO-listed slave house.
shortDescription: West African culture, music, and poignant colonial history.
visaNotes: Visa-free for many nationalities for up to 90 days.

name: Etosha National Park
description: A vast salt pan safari destination in Namibia where elephants, lions, rhinos, and hundreds of bird species converge at floodlit waterholes for dramatic night and day wildlife encounters.
shortDescription: Salt pan wildlife watching, floodlit waterholes, and big game.
visaNotes: Visa-free for many nationalities; always verify latest Namibia immigration rules.

name: Kruger National Park
description: One of Africa's largest game reserves, Kruger offers self-drive and guided safari experiences, a guaranteed Big Five sighting, and a wide range of accommodation from bush camps to ultra-luxury lodges.
shortDescription: Big Five safaris, self-drives, and world-class bush lodges.
visaNotes: Visa-free for many travelers; South Africa visa rules apply.

name: Mount Kilimanjaro
description: Africa's highest peak and the world's tallest freestanding mountain. Trekkers of varying fitness levels ascend through five distinct ecological zones to reach Uhuru Peak at 5,895 m.
shortDescription: Africa's roof — a bucket-list trek through five climate zones.
visaNotes: Standard Tanzanian visa rules apply.

name: Volcanoes National Park
description: Home to over a third of the world's mountain gorillas, this Rwandan park offers profoundly intimate wildlife encounters in misty Virunga volcano forests, paired with golden monkey tracking and cultural experiences.
shortDescription: Mountain gorilla trekking in Rwanda's misty Virunga volcanoes.
visaNotes: Rwanda offers visa-free entry to all African Union members and many other nationalities.

name: Sahara Desert
description: The world's largest hot desert stretching across 11 countries, offering camel treks through golden dunes of Erg Chebbi, stargazing under unpolluted skies, and authentic Berber camp experiences.
shortDescription: Camel treks, towering dunes, and a billion stars at night.
visaNotes: Visa requirements vary by country of entry; Morocco is visa-free for most nationalities.

name: Fez
description: Morocco's spiritual and intellectual capital, home to the world's oldest university and the largest living medieval city. The 9th-century medina of Fes el-Bali is a sensory maze of tanneries, madrasas, and artisan workshops.
shortDescription: Medieval medina, ancient tanneries, and Moroccan craftsmanship.
visaNotes: Visa-free for most nationalities for stays up to 90 days.

name: Okavango Delta
description: The world's largest inland delta, a UNESCO World Heritage Site where floodwaters transform the Kalahari into an extraordinary ecosystem teeming with elephants, hippos, leopards, and wild dogs, best explored by mokoro canoe.
shortDescription: Mokoro canoes, floodplain wildlife, and wild dog country.
visaNotes: Visa-free for most nationalities for stays up to 90 days.

name: Luxor
description: The world's greatest open-air museum, where the Valley of the Kings, Karnak Temple, and Luxor Temple sit along the Nile, offering an unmatched concentration of pharaonic monuments.
shortDescription: Valley of the Kings, Karnak Temple, and Nile felucca cruises.
visaNotes: E-visa or visa on arrival available for most nationalities.

name: Pyramids of Giza
description: The last surviving wonder of the ancient world. The Great Pyramid of Khufu, the Sphinx, and the Giza Necropolis deliver an incomparable encounter with human civilisation's most ambitious construction.
shortDescription: The last ancient wonder — pyramids, Sphinx, and desert mystique.
visaNotes: E-visa or visa on arrival available for most nationalities.

name: Chefchaouen
description: The 'Blue Pearl' of Morocco, a mountain medina of infinite blue-washed lanes, artisan shops, and Rif Mountain hikes that has become one of Africa's most Instagrammed destinations.
shortDescription: Blue-washed medina lanes, mountain air, and artisan markets.
visaNotes: Visa-free for most nationalities for stays up to 90 days.

name: Bazaruto Archipelago
description: A remote Indian Ocean archipelago of five pristine islands in Mozambique, protecting the last viable population of dugongs in East Africa and offering world-class diving, flamingo dunes, and exclusive island lodges.
shortDescription: Dugong sanctuary, flamingo dunes, and untouched Indian Ocean reef.
visaNotes: Visa on arrival available at Mozambique entry points for most nationalities.

name: Ouidah & Pendjari National Park
description: A dual experience combining Ouidah's living Voodoo culture and slave-trade heritage on the Slave Route with Pendjari's remote safari ecosystem, home to one of West Africa's last significant populations of lions, elephants, and hippos.
shortDescription: Voodoo heritage meets West Africa's wildest safari ecosystem.
visaNotes: E-visa required for most visitors; apply online before travel.

name: Simbavati Waterberg
description: A malaria-free safari retreat set within the Waterberg Biosphere Reserve, offering intimate Big Five encounters, horseback safaris, and sustainable luxury lodge experiences in a UNESCO-listed highland wilderness.
shortDescription: Malaria-free Big Five safaris in a UNESCO biosphere reserve.
visaNotes: Visa-free for many travelers; standard South Africa entry rules apply.

name: Meroë Pyramids
description: The forgotten pyramids of the ancient Kushite Kingdom, more numerous than Egypt's, rising steeply from red desert dunes near the Nile. Virtually crowd-free, they offer a profound off-the-beaten-path encounter with African antiquity.
shortDescription: More pyramids than Egypt, in the Sudanese desert, crowd-free.
visaNotes: Sudan requires a visa for most nationalities; check current situation before travel.

name: Tsingy de Bemaraha
description: A UNESCO World Heritage wilderness of razor-sharp limestone pinnacles in western Madagascar, accessible only by suspension bridges and via ferrata, sheltering unique lemur species and endemic flora found nowhere else on Earth.
shortDescription: Razor-sharp limestone labyrinths, lemurs, and Malagasy wilderness.
visaNotes: Tourist visa on arrival available for most nationalities at Madagascar entry points.
